text:
Rahel Daniel bizarrely wandered off the track while leading theOlympicwomen’s 10,000m final to baffleBBCcommentators atParis2024.

Daniel looked comfortable at the 2,000m mark – ahead of the pack – but then stumbled, raised her hand to her face and spat before walking off.

The Eritrean tried to exit the Stade de France, only for Olympic officials to block the 22-year-old from leaving, and making her walk around the side of the circuit.

It is unconfirmed why Daniel wandered off during the race but it has been suggested that humidity problems were to blame for her early withdrawal.

BBC commentators were left confused why Daniel had ‘wandered off’ as the gold medal eventually went to Kenya’s Beatrice Chebet.

Daniel finished fifth at the World Athletics Championships two years ago and had high hopes heading into the 10,000m final.

But she was not the only runner to pull out mid-race with Frenchwoman Alessia Zarbo exiting after falling over, which resulted in gasps from the home crowd.

The bronze medal went to Dutchwoman Sifan Hassan and Italy’s Nadia Battocletti took silver with Kenya’s Chebet finishing ahead of both of them.

Chebet, 24, who was also crowned 5,000m champion on Monday, said: ‘This 10,000m gold medal, I can describe it as the most important medal in my life.

‘I came here targeting the 10,000m gold medal, not a gold in 5,000m.

‘To do the 5,000m and 10,000m is not something easy. But just focus, and know that you can achieve. Just believe in yourself. I believed that I can do it.

‘My country [was] watching me, so I had to focus on the 10,000m and not think that I’ve won a gold medal [in the 5,000m]. That was my target.’

title: 10,000m runner bizarrely wanders off track at Paris Olympics midway through race | Metro News
description: 10,000m runner Rahel Daniel bizarrely wandered off the track at the 2024 Paris Olympics midway through a race to baffle BBC commentators.
